---
title: "Final Ananlysis"
author: "Nabin , NAVA"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE}
library(cfbfastR)
library(tidyverse)
```

### College Football Data Extraction
```{r import-cfb-data}
# Pull schedule data from espn_cfb_schedule function

years = c(2000:2005)
data_func <- function(years) {
    espn_cfb_schedule(year = years)
}
schedule <- map_dfr(years, data_func)
```

### Crime Data Extraction
```{r import-crime-data}
colleges <- read_csv("colleges.csv")
offenses <- read_csv("offenses.csv")
unfiltered_ori_and_team_names <- read_csv("unfiltered_ori_and_team_names.csv", col_names = c("ori", "team_name"))
```

### Filter College Data To Out 26 Teams
```{r filter-colleges}
filtered_schedule <- schedule %>%
    filter(home_team_full %in% colleges$NCAA_Team | 
           away_team_full %in% colleges$NCAA_Team)

filtered_schedule <- filtered_schedule %>% 
    select(matchup,
           season,
           game_id,
           game_date,
           home_team_full,
           home_team_name,
           away_team_full,
           away_team_name,
           home_win,
           away_win)
```

### Remove Duplicate ORIs
```{r deciding-duplicate-ORIs}

# Namaes of teams that have > 1 ORI value
duplicate_team_names <- unfiltered_ori_and_team_names %>%
    group_by(team_name) %>%
    summarise(count = n()) %>%
    filter(count > 1)%>%
    select(team_name)

# Names and ORIs of teams that have > 1 ORI value
duplicate_ori <- unfiltered_ori_and_team_names %>%
    filter(team_name %in% duplicate_team_names$team_name) 

# ORIs of teams that have > 1 ORI value with the associated number of offenses
offense_counts_by_duplicate_ori <- offenses %>% 
    filter(ori %in% duplicate_ori$ori)%>%
    group_by(ori)%>%
    summarise(count = n())

# Choose the ORI value with the highest number of offenses
filtered_duplicates <- full_join(offense_counts_by_duplicate_ori, duplicate_ori, by = 'ori') %>%
    filter(is.na(count) == FALSE) %>% # The NA values mean there're no offenses associated with that ORI
    group_by(team_name) %>% 
    filter(count == max(count))

# Create a new CSV file with no duplicates
unfiltered_ori_and_team_names %>%
    filter(!(ori %in% duplicate_ori$ori) | (ori %in% filtered_duplicates$ori) ) %>%  # Keep rows who don't have duplicates + Add the filtered duplicates
    write_csv("ori_and_team_names.csv")

```

### Join Crime and College Football Data
```{r joining-crime-and-cfb}
ori_and_team_names <- read_csv("ori_and_team_names.csv")
offenses <- offenses %>%
            filter(ori %in% ori_and_team_names$ori)

offenses%>%group_by(incident_date,ori)
filtered_schedule
# UP TO HERE ---------------------------------------------------------------------
filtered_schedule <- filtered_schedule %>% 
    pivot_longer(names_to='home/away',
                cols = c(home_team_full,away_team_full),
                values_to = "team_name")
ori_with_schedule%>%group_by(incident_date,ori)
ori_with_schedule<-full_join(filtered_schedule, ori_and_team_names,by = "team_name")#%>%#select(ori,team_name)
ori_with_schedule <-ori_with_schedule%>%rename(incident_date="game_date")
ori_with_schedule
offenses
ori_and_team_names
test_frame<-full_join(ori_with_schedule,offenses,by=c('ori','incident_date'))
test_frame%>%view()
test_frame<-full_join(filtered_schedule, final_ori_team_names, by = "team_name")%>%view()
```
```{r}
offenses%>%filter(year(incident_date)<=2001)%>%ggplot(aes(x=incident_date))+geom_histogram()
```
